---
title: "Lock R version"
author: "Clinton"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Even with renv, installing R using apt on ubuntu server often leads to dashboard breaks whenever there's an update on system packages. To avoid that, its best practise to lock the R version and do scheduled updates manually. 

Here's how to do that:

## Remove all APT-managed R 
Its good to eliminate conflicts before installing from source

```{bash}

sudo apt remove --purge -y r-base r-base-core r-recommended r-base-dev
sudo apt autoremove -y

```

## Install build dependencies
You will still need to install system libraries using apt. 

```{bash}

sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y \
  build-essential \
  gcc g++ gfortran \
  libreadline-dev \
  libx11-dev libxt-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev \
  libbz2-dev liblzma-dev \
  libpcre2-dev \
  zlib1g-dev \
  libblas-dev liblapack-dev \
  libicu-dev \
  texinfo \
  perl \
wget
  
```

## Download the exact R version
I will use R version 4.3.3. Replace with your desired version. 

```{bash}

cd /usr/local/src
sudo wget https://cran.r-project.org/src/base/R-4/R-4.3.3.tar.gz
sudo tar -xvzf R-4.3.3.tar.gz
cd R-4.3.3

```

## Configure for server
```{bash}

./configure \
  --prefix=/opt/R/4.3.3 \
  --enable-R-shlib \
  --with-x=no
  
```

## Compile and install
```{bash}

make -j$(nproc)
sudo make install

```

## Lock R installation
You can now explicitly control the version via PATH
```{bash}

sudo nano /etc/profile.d/r-4.3.3.sh

export R_HOME=/opt/R/4.3.3/lib/R
export PATH=/opt/R/4.3.3/bin:$PATH

source /etc/profile.d/r-4.3.3.sh

```
